metadata.title: Therapeutic Area Benchmarks — Protocol Quality Standards
metadata.description: Evidence-based benchmarks by therapeutic area for protocol quality assessment, including typical endpoints, sample sizes, duration, and success rates from published clinical trials.

typical_sample_size.note: Varies by effect size and event rate

common_primary_endpoints: Objective Response Rate (ORR) — for accelerated approval
phase_3_benchmarks.response_criteria: RECIST v1.1 (Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors)

comparator_standards.second_line_plus: Investigator's choice or best available therapy
comparator_standards.note: Placebo alone rarely acceptable unless add-on design

special_considerations: Delayed separation of KM curves expected
Restricted mean survival time (RMST) may be more appropriate than HR when non-proportional hazards

phase_3_benchmarks.primary_endpoint: 3-point MACE (CV death + non-fatal MI + non-fatal stroke)
design_requirements: Independent Clinical Events Committee (CEC) for adjudication

success_rates.note: Higher than oncology due to established endpoints

primary_endpoints: CV death + HF hospitalisation composite
NT-proBNP change from baseline
KCCQ (Kansas City Cardiomyopathy Questionnaire)

biomarker_requirements: Amyloid PET positivity OR CSF Aβ42/p-tau ratio
design_considerations: Disease modification vs symptomatic claims — different endpoint and duration requirements
Mandatory biomarker confirmation of AD pathology
Long duration (≥18 months) for disease modification
High screen failure rate (30-50%) due to biomarker requirements

success_rates.note: Historically very low; improving with biomarker-selected populations

primary_endpoints: Annualised relapse rate (ARR)
12-week confirmed disability progression (CDP)
MRI: T2 lesion volume, gadolinium-enhancing lesions
ACR20/50/70 response rates
DAS28-CRP remission (< 2.6)
HAQ-DI (function)
rheumatoid_arthritis.design: Randomised, double-blind, with rescue therapy allowed

primary_endpoints: IGA 0/1 with ≥2-grade improvement
EASI-75 (75% improvement in EASI score)

design_flexibility: Crossover designs to maximise information per patient
endpoint_considerations: Biomarker endpoints acceptable for accelerated/conditional approval
Composite endpoints to increase statistical power
Clinician Global Impression of Change (CGI-C) accepted

regulatory_incentives.FDA: Orphan Drug Act: 7 years exclusivity, tax credits, fee waivers
regulatory_incentives.EMA: Orphan designation: 10 years market exclusivity, protocol assistance, fee reductions
regulatory_incentives.PMDA: Orphan designation: 10 years exclusivity, priority review, tax credits

primary_endpoints: HbA1c change from baseline at 24-26 weeks

cv_safety_requirement.description: FDA requires CVOT meta-analysis or dedicated CVOT
cv_safety_requirement.threshold: Upper bound of 95% CI for MACE HR must be <1.8 pre-approval, <1.3 post-approval

diabetes_type_2.design: Add-on to metformin or active comparator

primary_endpoints: % change in body weight from baseline
Proportion achieving ≥5% weight loss
obesity.co_primary: Often mean % weight change + responder analysis

typical_duration_months.note: 12 weeks for bronchodilator, 52 weeks for controller

primary_endpoints: FEV1 improvement from baseline (bronchodilators)
Annualised exacerbation rate reduction (controllers)
design_standards: Randomised, double-blind, parallel group
Step-down design for steroid-sparing claims
Add-on to standard background therapy
Minimum 12 weeks for bronchodilator efficacy
Minimum 52 weeks for exacerbation prevention
Severity stratification: mild, moderate, severe, uncontrolled

comparator_standards.bronchodilators: Add-on to ICS, active comparator preferred

regulatory_expectations.FDA: FEV1 + exacerbation rate, population-specific biomarker (eosinophils, FeNO, IgE)
regulatory_expectations.EMA: EMA/CHMP guideline: FEV1, exacerbation rate, QoL; biomarker-defined subgroups

primary_endpoints: Trough FEV1 (bronchodilators)
Exacerbation rate reduction (anti-inflammatory)
All-cause mortality (CVOT-style for some agents)
design_standards: GOLD classification for severity stratification
Active comparator preferred (LABA, LAMA, ICS/LABA)
Minimum 12 weeks for bronchodilator efficacy
Minimum 52 weeks for exacerbation prevention

primary_endpoints: Clinical cure at test-of-cure visit
Microbiological eradication
28-day all-cause mortality (for serious infections like HAP/VAP)
design_standards: Non-inferiority design with FDA/EMA-agreed margin (typically 10% or 15%)
Enroll patients with confirmed pathogen (randomise on clinical diagnosis, analyse microbiological per-pathogen)
PK/PD target attainment analysis
Site-specific infection trials: ABSSSI, CABP, cUTI, cIAI, HAP/VAP, BSI
Drug-resistant pathogen enrichment strategy

regulatory_expectations.FDA: FDA LPAD pathway for limited population antibacterials; streamlined program for qualified infectious disease product (QIDP)
regulatory_expectations.EMA: EMA addendum on antibacterials: PK/PD modeling, resistance monitoring
regulatory_expectations.global: AMR (Antimicrobial Resistance) incentives: transferable exclusivity vouchers (EU), QIDP designation (FDA)

primary_endpoints: HIV-1 RNA <50 copies/mL at Week 48 (FDA Snapshot analysis)
HIV-1 RNA <200 copies/mL (secondary)
design_standards: Non-inferiority design vs standard of care ART regimen
48-week primary analysis, 96-week supportive
Switch study design for treatment-experienced patients
FDA snapshot algorithm for primary analysis
Both treatment-naive and treatment-experienced populations

primary_endpoints: SVR12 (Sustained Virological Response at 12 weeks post-treatment) for HCV
HBsAg loss (functional cure) for HBV
design_notes: HCV: Non-inferiority to SOC DAA regimen, SVR12 near 95-100%
HBV: Functional cure (HBsAg loss) rare with current therapies; new endpoints emerging

primary_endpoints: Clinical remission: adapted Mayo score (stool frequency subscore ≤1, rectal bleeding subscore 0, endoscopic subscore ≤1)
design_standards: Induction (8-12 weeks) + Maintenance (52 weeks) study design
Placebo-controlled for induction; re-randomisation or continuous treatment for maintenance
Central endoscopy reading mandatory
Moderate-to-severe UC population (adapted Mayo 6-12)
Biologic-experienced subgroup analysis

primary_endpoints: Clinical remission: CDAI <150 or PRO-2 remission (stool frequency ≤1.5/day, abdominal pain ≤1)
Endoscopic response: SES-CD reduction ≥50% from baseline
design_standards: PRO-based endpoints replacing CDAI per FDA guidance
Central endoscopy reading
Induction + maintenance design
Biologic-failure population: enrichment or stratification

primary_endpoints: Resolution of steatohepatitis (NAS improvement) without worsening of fibrosis
Improvement in liver fibrosis by ≥1 stage without worsening of steatohepatitis
secondary_endpoints: Change in liver stiffness by elastography
design_standards: Histological assessment via paired liver biopsy (baseline + end-of-treatment)
Phase III may use conditional approval pathway with histological endpoints, confirmatory study with clinical outcomes
FDA surrogate endpoint: fibrosis improvement or NASH resolution (accelerated approval basis)
Long-term outcome trials: 4-8+ years for liver-related clinical events
Central pathology reading mandatory

primary_endpoints: BCVA (Best Corrected Visual Acuity) change from baseline in ETDRS letters
Proportion gaining ≥15 letters BCVA
design_standards: ETDRS chart for visual acuity assessment (masked examiners)
OCT central reading centre
Active comparator (anti-VEGF) for wet AMD, DME
Sham injection control with mandatory rescue criteria for ethics
Non-inferiority margin: typically 4-5 ETDRS letters
Treatment interval comparison (e.g., treat-and-extend vs fixed dosing)

therapeutic_areas.DME: Anti-VEGF comparator; stratify by baseline BCVA
therapeutic_areas.geographic_atrophy: Anatomic endpoint (GA lesion growth rate by FAF)

typical_sample_size.note: High placebo response requires larger samples

primary_endpoints: Change from baseline in MADRS or HAM-D total score at Week 6-8
design_standards: Placebo-controlled (active comparator reference arm optional but recommended)
High placebo response rate (30-40%) — major design challenge
Strategies to reduce placebo response: single-blind placebo lead-in, centralised rating, SIGMA rater training
6-8 week acute treatment phase
Suicidality monitoring: C-SSRS at every visit (FDA requirement)
Treatment-resistant depression (TRD): STAR*D-aligned staging

regulatory_expectations.FDA: Two adequate well-controlled studies; suicidality boxed warning for <25 years; C-SSRS mandatory
regulatory_expectations.EMA: Active comparator recommended; relapse prevention study for long-term efficacy

primary_endpoints: Change from baseline in PANSS total score at Week 4-6
design_standards: Placebo-controlled, 4-6 week acute phase
PANSS and CGI-S as co-primary or key secondary
Long-acting injectable (LAI) studies: relapse prevention design (randomised withdrawal)
Metabolic monitoring: weight, glucose, lipids, prolactin
EPS monitoring: AIMS, Barnes, Simpson-Angus scales

primary_endpoints: Disease-specific functional endpoint
Biomarker-based endpoint (e.g., factor VIII levels for haemophilia)
Time to event (e.g., vaso-occlusive crises for SCD)
design_standards: Single-arm with historical/natural history comparator common
Intra-patient comparison (pre-treatment vs post-treatment)
Long-term follow-up: 15 years for integrating vectors (FDA), 5 years minimum for non-integrating
Dose selection based on non-clinical and Phase I/II data
Risk monitoring: insertional mutagenesis, immunogenicity, off-target effects
Manufacturing: vein-to-vein process, turnaround time, potency testing
Conditional/accelerated approval pathways commonly used

regulatory_expectations.FDA: RMAT designation, BLA pathway, long-term follow-up per FDA gene therapy guidance
regulatory_expectations.EMA: ATMP regulation, CAT assessment, conditional MA common, 15-year safety follow-up

success_rates.note: Improving with better vector technology; manufacturing remains key challenge

primary_endpoints: Overall response rate (ORR) for single-arm (accelerated approval)
Event-free survival (EFS) for randomised vs SOC
design_standards: Single-arm with historical comparator or randomised vs SOC
CRS grading: ASTCT consensus scale
ICANS (neurotoxicity) grading and management protocol
Bridging therapy during manufacturing period
Manufacturing success rate reporting (% of patients receiving product)
Long-term follow-up: secondary malignancy monitoring (T-cell lymphoma — FDA class-wide boxed warning 2024)
REMS may be required for CRS/ICANS

primary_endpoints: Vaccine efficacy (VE): 1 - (attack rate vaccinated / attack rate placebo)
Prevention of confirmed disease (PCR-confirmed or clinical criteria)
secondary_endpoints: Immunogenicity: seroconversion rate, GMT (Geometric Mean Titre)
design_standards: Randomised, observer-blind, placebo-controlled (or active comparator for established vaccines)
Event-driven design with pre-specified VE threshold
FDA generally requires VE ≥50% (lower bound of 95% CI >30%)
Immunobridging: approved in some settings to extrapolate to new populations based on immune response
Lot-to-lot consistency studies for manufacturing
Co-administration studies with routine vaccines
Safety: large safety database (≥3000 vaccinated; FDA often requires larger), follow-up for rare adverse events
DSMB mandatory with pre-specified interim analyses

regulatory_expectations.FDA: Pre-EUA/BLA meeting, FDA VRBPAC advisory committee review, VAERS post-marketing surveillance
regulatory_expectations.EMA: CHMP assessment, conditional MA pathway available, PASS for long-term safety
regulatory_expectations.WHO: WHO prequalification for global access; WHO Target Product Profile alignment

primary_endpoints: Composite: sustained ≥40-57% eGFR decline, ESKD (dialysis or transplant), or renal death
Time to kidney failure (ESKD)
secondary_endpoints: eGFR slope (rate of decline over time)
CV events (many CKD trials include CV composite)
design_standards: Event-driven design with adjudication committee
Enrichment for high-risk CKD (eGFR 25-60 mL/min, UACR >200 mg/g)
Active control or standard of care background therapy
eGFR slope as surrogate endpoint for accelerated approval per FDA guidance
Long follow-up: 2-4+ years for renal outcome events
FDA 2022 guidance: eGFR endpoints for accelerated approval in CKD

primary_endpoints: Change in NRS (Numerical Rating Scale) pain intensity from baseline
Proportion with ≥30% or ≥50% pain reduction (responder analysis)
design_standards: Enriched enrollment randomised withdrawal (EERW) design accepted by FDA
Parallel group placebo-controlled design (EMA preference)
High placebo response: 30-40% (similar challenge to psychiatry)
Abuse-deterrent formulation assessment for opioids
Suicidality monitoring for chronic pain trials (C-SSRS)
FDA encourages non-opioid alternatives

quality_scoring_benchmarks.description: Scoring thresholds used by NIX AI for protocol quality assessment

excellent.description: Publication-ready protocol meeting all ICH/regional requirements with comprehensive endpoints and statistics

good.description: Solid protocol with minor gaps that can be addressed in amendments

adequate.description: Meets minimum requirements but has notable deficiencies

needs_improvement.description: Significant gaps in design, endpoints, or methodology
